description_long: This cowshed features a rectangular structure with a prominent gable roof covering the main building and an attached open-sided extension with a transparent or slatted roof, likely for additional shelter or feeding space. The main building is constructed from vertical wooden planks, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The roof is pitched and made of a solid, likely metal or composite material, while the extension's roof is supported by evenly spaced posts and appears semi-transparent, possibly made from polycarbonate or slatted wood. The front of the cowshed has a wide, open entrance, suitable for livestock movement, and the side extension is open, providing ample ventilation and light. No windows are visible, but the open design of the extension and the large entrance ensure good airflow. The overall style is functional and rural, designed for practicality and ease of access for both animals and caretakers.
description: A rustic cowshed with a gable roof, wooden plank walls, and an open-sided extension with a semi-transparent roof, designed for livestock shelter and ventilation.
description_view_0: The main gable roof and wide open entrance are visible, with the attached open-sided extension extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the open extension and the supporting posts beneath the semi-transparent roof.
description_view_2: Frontal view showing the full width of the cowshed, the open extension with multiple support posts, and the main building behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the extension's structure and the solid main building with its pitched roof.
attribute_roof_type: gable with attached shed roof extension
attribute_door_style: wide open entrance, likely sliding or double hinged wooden doors (not visible but implied by structure)
